name: agentorchestra-scripts
description: One-click terminal commands for AgentOrchestra on Windows.  Each entry has a `step` field that gives the typical first-time-to-running order (1 → install, 2/3 → verify CLI auth, 4 → launch, 5 → stop, 6 → update, 7 → diagnose, 8 → wipe-and-retry).  `ops` (the Operator Panel itself) sits outside the sequence — it's the host that exposes the rest.  Double-click any .cmd from File Explorer, or right-click → Send to → Desktop (create shortcut) for one-click access.

commands.label: Operator Panel
commands.summary: Tiny GUI window with one button per command in this folder + a live output pane.  Reads this manifest, so any command added here shows up automatically.
commands.when_to_run: Make this your everyday desktop shortcut.  All the steps below are reachable from inside this panel.

commands.label: Step 1 — First-time install
commands.summary: Create the virtual environment and install AgentOrchestra + its [gui] extras.  Idempotent.  Re-run after a Python upgrade or if .venv goes missing.
commands.when_to_run: Once, the first time you clone the repo.  Or any time you nuked .venv.

commands.label: Step 2 — Verify Claude Code CLI
commands.summary: Smoke-test the local `claude` binary: confirm it's on PATH, then send `claude -p "say hi in 5 words"` to verify the Max-plan login is working in headless mode.
commands.when_to_run: Right after Step 1, and any time a Claude card stops replying.  If it says "Not logged in", run `claude` interactively and `/login`.

commands.label: Step 3 — Verify Gemini CLI
commands.summary: Smoke-test the local `gemini` binary the same way: PATH check + a headless `gemini -p "..."` call to verify your Gemini CLI auth.
commands.when_to_run: Right after Step 2.  If auth fails, run `gemini` interactively to sign in.  Skip if you only use Claude.

commands.label: Step 4 — Start AgentOrchestra
commands.summary: Open the GUI.  The service is auto-spawned in the background by the GUI itself if 127.0.0.1:8765 is free.
commands.when_to_run: Every session.  This is the everyday verb.

commands.label: Step 5 — Stop AgentOrchestra
commands.summary: Close the GUI window and any background service it supervised.
commands.when_to_run: When you close the laptop or want to free port 8765.

commands.label: Step 6 — Pull latest + refresh deps
commands.summary: git pull --ff-only origin main, then pip install -e .[gui] --upgrade.
commands.when_to_run: After GitHub Desktop's Pull origin, when you've changed branch, or before reporting a bug.

commands.label: Step 7 — Environment diagnostic
commands.summary: Print a one-page health report: Python version, .venv status, claude / gemini on PATH, port 8765, local data directory, annotator import, AgentOrchestra version.
commands.when_to_run: When something's wrong.  Copy/paste the output into a bug report.

commands.label: Step 8 — Wipe local state
commands.summary: Delete %USERPROFILE%\.local\share\agentorchestra (SQLite store, first-run sentinel, annotation logs).  Does NOT touch the repo, git history, or CLI auth.
commands.when_to_run: Last resort, when the local DB is wedged and you want a clean slate.  Confirms before deleting.
